title: Step 1: Add Reset Button UI to Maintenance Management (no logic)

requirements.text: Add a 'Reset' button to the MaintenanceManagement component UI. The button should be visible only to admin users (consistent with the existing admin-only 'Add Maintenance Expense' button pattern). The button should be styled with a destructive/warning style (e.g., red or orange) to indicate it is a dangerous action. The button should render in the header/controls area of the maintenance management section. The button must have no click handler logic attached yet — it should be inert (or log a placeholder console message). Do not add any backend calls or state changes.

quotes: Step 1: Reset button UI only — Add a 'Reset' button to the maintenance management section — no logic attached yet, just the visual element.

acceptanceCriteria: A 'Reset' button is visible in the MaintenanceManagement component when the logged-in user is an admin.
The button is NOT visible to non-admin (viewer) users.
The button uses a destructive or warning visual style (red/orange tones) to signal danger.
The button is placed near the existing admin controls (e.g., next to or below the 'Add Maintenance Expense' button).
Clicking the button does not trigger any backend call or data mutation — it is a no-op or logs a placeholder console message.
No new backend functions or mutations are introduced in this step.
The rest of the MaintenanceManagement UI remains unchanged.

constraints: Do not add any backend logic, Motoko functions, or mutations in this step.
Do not wire the button to any existing or new query/mutation hooks.
Preserve all existing MaintenanceManagement functionality exactly as-is.
Do not modify any immutable paths (frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui).
nonGoals: Wiring the button to any reset logic (Steps 4 and 5).
Any changes to other components outside MaintenanceManagement.
